text: Planets don't orbit in perfect circles — they dance in ellipses. In the early 1600s, Johannes Kepler cracked the code of planetary movement. He discovered that planets sweep out equal areas in equal times. Imagine a line from the sun to a planet. It covers the same area in a given time, whether it’s close or far. This means planets move faster when they're nearer to the sun — gravity really is a game changer! Curious about more cosmic mysteries? Like and follow for more!
